title: Configure and verify DHCP client and relay

questions.question: Which command can verify the active DHCP server that has assigned an IP address to the router?
choices: Router#show dhcp lease
Router#show ip dhcp lease
Router#show ip lease
Router#show ip interface

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Router#show dhcp lease".

incorrect.explanation: **Router#show ip dhcp lease** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Router#show ip lease** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Router#show ip interface** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: Which statement is true about the Offer packet of DHCP?
choices: The layer 3 destination is a unicast to the DHCP client.
The layer 2 destination is the MAC address of the DHCP client.
The layer 2 source is the MAC address of the server.
The layer 3 source is a link-local address of the client.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "The layer 2 source is the MAC address of the server.".

incorrect.explanation: Switches learn from the **source** MAC on the ingress port. The destination MAC is used later to look up where to forward the frame — it is not what gets recorded during learning.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing source MAC (learning) with destination MAC (forwarding lookup)

incorrect.explanation: **The layer 2 destination is the MAC address of the DHCP client.** is not the behavior described here. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing source MAC (learning) with destination MAC (forwarding lookup)

incorrect.explanation: MAC learning is a **Layer 2** process. Switches read Ethernet frame addresses, not IP headers, when populating the MAC address table.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Expecting static NAT to serve many hosts — Static NAT is 1:1; use PAT/overload for many-to-one

questions.question: Which command configure a DHCP relay agent on an interface to the DHCP server of 10.10.1.101?
choices: Router(config)#ip dhcp server 10.10.1.101
Router(config-if)#ip dhcp server 10.10.1.101
Router(config-if)#ip relay-agent 10.10.1.101
Router(config-if)#ip helper-address 10.10.1.101

incorrect.explanation: **Router(config)#ip dhcp server 10.10.1.101** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Router(config-if)#ip dhcp server 10.10.1.101** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Router(config-if)#ip relay-agent 10.10.1.101** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: Which DHCP field helps a DHCP server decide which scope to serve to the DHCP relay agent?
choices: CIADDR
GIADDR
SIADDR
CHADDR

incorrect.explanation: **CIADDR** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **SIADDR** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **CHADDR** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question:
Network layout:
- Host A: 192.168.10.10/24 on VLAN 10 (no local DHCP server)
- Router R1 Gi0/0: 192.168.10.1 — needs ip helper-address toward remote DHCP
- Remote DHCP server: 10.0.0.5 on another subnet

what is required on the network to allow Host A to receive an IP address from the existing DHCP server?
choices: A second DHCP server on the Host A network
A DHCP relay agent on Router B interface Gi0/0
A DHCP relay agent on Switch A
A DHCP relay agent on Router A interface Gi0/0

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "A DHCP relay agent on Router A interface Gi0/0".

incorrect.explanation: **A second DHCP server on the Host A network** does not answer what is in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **A DHCP relay agent on Router B interface Gi0/0** does not answer what is in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **A DHCP relay agent on Switch A** does not answer what is in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: Which command can diagnose DHCP relay agent messages on a router or switch?
choices: Router#debug dhcp
Router#show ip dhcp detail
Router#debug ip dhcp server packet
Router#debug ip dhcp

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Router#debug ip dhcp server packet".

incorrect.explanation: **Router#debug dhcp** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Router#show ip dhcp detail** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Router#debug ip dhcp** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: Identify DHCPv6 used for when a network is configured for Stateless Address Autoconfiguration (SLAAC)?
choices: Stateful configuration of clients for IPv6 addressing
Configuration of clients with the IPv6 network IDs
Configuration of clients with IPv6 options
Stateless configuration of clients for IPv6 addressing

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Configuration of clients with IPv6 options".

incorrect.explanation: **Stateful configuration of clients for IPv6 addressing** does not answer configure in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Configuration of clients with the IPv6 network IDs** does not answer configure in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Stateless configuration of clients for IPv6 addressing** does not answer configure in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: The DHCP server in the network went down. What happens to clients that have obtained IP addresses from the DHCP server?
choices: They lose their IP address immediately.
They lose their IP address after one-half of their lease has expired.
They lose their IP address after seven-eighths of their lease has expired.
They lose their IP address after their entire lease has expired.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "They lose their IP address after their entire lease has expired.".

incorrect.explanation: **They lose their IP address immediately.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **They lose their IP address after one-half of their lease has expired.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **They lose their IP address after seven-eighths of their lease has expired.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: Which statement is correct about stateful DHCPv6?
choices: Stateful DHCPv6 supplies the network ID and host ID.
Stateful DHCPv6 supplies the network ID, host ID, and default router.
Stateful DHCPv6 communicates via broadcasts.
Stateful DHCPv6 works in conjunction with SLAAC.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Stateful DHCPv6 supplies the network ID and host ID.".

incorrect.explanation: **Stateful DHCPv6 supplies the network ID, host ID, and default router.** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Stateful DHCPv6 communicates via broadcasts.** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **Stateful DHCPv6 works in conjunction with SLAAC.** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: Which command configure a router interface to obtain its IP address via a stateful DHCPv6?
choices: RouterA(config)#ipv6 address dhcp gi 0/0
RouterA(config-if)#ipv6 address dhcpv6
RouterA(config-if)#ipv6 address dhcp
RouterA(config)#ipv6 address stateless

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "RouterA(config-if)#ipv6 address dhcp".

incorrect.explanation: **RouterA(config)#ipv6 address dhcp gi 0/0** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **RouterA(config-if)#ipv6 address dhcpv6** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **RouterA(config)#ipv6 address stateless** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: What happens if you delete a current lease on the DHCP server?
choices: The server will contact the client to immediately relinquish the IP address.
The client will immediately renew its lease for the current IP address.
The server will offer the IP address to another node, which will cause a duplicate address.
The server will offer the IP address to another node, at which time the original client will relinquish the IP address.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "The server will offer the IP address to another node, which will cause a duplicate address.".

incorrect.explanation: **The server will contact the client to immediately relinquish the IP address.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **The client will immediately renew its lease for the current IP address.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: DHCP DORA order matters — identify which message the stem describes before picking a step.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Misordering or mislabeling DHCP messages

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.

questions.question: What happens at the client when the lease for an IP address reaches seven-eighths of the lease cycle?
choices: The DHCP client will perform a DHCP rebinding.
Nothing. The DHCP client will retain the lease.
The DHCP client will renew its lease.
The DHCP client will relinquish the use of the IP address.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "The DHCP client will perform a DHCP rebinding.".

incorrect.explanation: **Nothing. The DHCP client will retain the lease.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **The DHCP client will renew its lease.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

incorrect.explanation: **The DHCP client will relinquish the use of the IP address.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Confusing DHCP server, relay agent, and client roles

answerReview.examTip: DHCP is **DORA** — match the message name to the step the stem describes.
